english: Ssk. sea-monster.
1. Lt. = mtsan-dbye (mtshan dbye). - 2. Mecca Stg.
v. ma (ma) 1.
v. ma-rgán (ma rgan).
Wdn., W. poplar-tree.
v. ma (ma) II.
*mar-gád* (mar rgad) Glr., from मरकत, emerald.
W. *mar-gán* (mar rgan) 1. matron, grandam. 2. C. also *ma-kén* (ma mkhan) cook; quarter-master.
1. v. ma (ma) I. - 2. head-cook.

wylie: ma tri mu tri sa la 'dzu
english: is said to be a form of prayer of the Bonpos, as the Ommanipadmehūm is of the Buddhists; Desg. p. 242 has: ma tchri mou me sa le gou.
Ld. a place on the roof of a house cleared for spreading grain there.
W. *mag-dán* (mag gdan), C. *ma-dén* (ma gdan) ground, basis, foundation; also for ma-ydán-gyi (ma gdan gyi) ri-mo (ri mo) ground-plan.
*ma-dú* (ma du) W. thorn, prickle, má-rdu-čan (ma rdu can) thorny, prickly.
*mar-do* (mar do) W. prob. a careless pronunciation of rmáṅ-rdo (rmang rdo).

1. without sexual distinction Med. and Gram. - 2. impotent, unable to beget S.g. - 3. barren, childless Wdn. (explained by bu-tsa-méd-pa (bu tsha med pa)). - 4. Cs.: also hermaphrodite, Wdn. however denotes this explicitly by mtsan-yñyis-pa (mtshan gnyis pa).
Med.? Cs: = मनु, मनस, yid (yid); as symb. num.: 14.
a medicine Wdn.; in Lh. Bryonia dioeca.

(Ssk. precious stone) 1. abbrev. of Ommanipadmehūm; *má-ṇi táṅ-če* (ma Ni btang ce) W. 1. to mutter prayers. 2. to purr like a cat. Hence 2. praying-cylinder, prop. ma-ṇi-čós-_kór (ma Ni chos 'khor) Schl. 230. - 3. consecrated stone-heaps or stone-walls (Mongul Obo) Schl. 196; ma-ṇi bka-_búm (ma Ni bka' 'bum) title of a book; as to its contents v. Schl. 84.
Mil., ma-pám (ma pham) Cs. = ma-drós-pa (ma dros pa), v. drós-pa (dros pa).

children's nurse Dzl., Glr., Cs.: nú-ma (nu ma) snún-pai (snun pa'i) wet-nurse, dri-ma (dri ma) _pyí-bai ('phyi ba'i) nurse for cleaning, púṅ-du (phung du) _kúr-bai ('khur ba'i) for carrying, rtséd-grogs-kyi (rtsed grogs kyi) for playing.
Ld. col. for na-bún (na bun), fog.
1. Sch. grandmother. - 2. Sch. ewe, sheep that has lambed. - 3. Mil. and elsewh. frq., a kind of wicked demons.
Lt. medicinal plant (?).
v. źú-ba (zhu ba).
W. *máb-źi* (mab zhi) 1. ground-work, basis, elementary principle, component part; prime colour; principal thing, main point. - 2. Sch. originally (?).
Ssk. = Tib. sgyu-_prul-ma (sgyu 'phrul ma) 'delusion', n. of the mother of Buddha Śākyamuni.
= tab-ryóg (thab ryog) kitchen-boy, scullion W.
v. ma (ma) II.
n. of a castle, perh. = rmé-ru (rme ru).
1. n. of a medicine Med. - 2. n. of a country Pth.
Sch. excellent! capital! - In Feer Introd. p. 69 it was explained by our Lama as = 'é-ma ('e ma) ah, well! Also Feer has: Eh bien!
Ld. snake-charmer, conjurer.
the western Ghauts famous for sandal-wood; the tracts along their foot, Malayalim, Malabar.
Ld. small lizard.
Ld. somerset; *má-lag lóg-če* (ma lag log ce) to perform a somerset, to play the tricks of a mountebank; to roll on the ground with legs turned up, of horses etc.
